commands.review_summary:
The PR updates ACP-core non-Error cause stringification to fall back to an object tag when `JSON.stringify` returns `undefined`, and adds a colocated Vitest regression test.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +19. Total +21 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the helper-level bug: current main returns `JSON.stringify(value)` directly, and a Node contract check shows that functions, symbols, and `undefined` serialize to `undefined`. I did not rerun an end-to-end ACP runtime flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair lane is needed; this PR already contains the concrete code and test change and now needs ordinary maintainer review.

target.title: fix(cli): add IPv4 loopback host to isPortBusy
target.body:
Closes #94426

## What Problem This Solves

`isPortBusy()` used a host-less probe (IPv6-first default) which missed IPv4-only port occupants on dual-stack hosts. Port detection returned `free` for an occupied port when the listener was IPv4-only.

## Why This Change Was Made

Changed `isPortBusy` to use `checkPortInUse` from `ports-inspect.ts` which probes with explicit host resolution, correctly detecting IPv4-only occupants.

## Evidence

```text
$ npx tsx -e 'import { createServer } from "node:net"; import { checkPortInUse } from "./src/infra/ports-inspect.ts"; ...'

checkPortInUse result:
busy
```

An IPv4-only listener on port 19876 is correctly detected as `busy` by `checkPortInUse`.
